VAL-CHROME-001.evidence: Screenshot shows no status bar at bottom. Bottom rows (y=795-799) have pixel values 13-27, matching the dark content area gradient. No bright bar, no text, no chrome at bottom. Content area extends seamlessly to y=799 (bottom edge).
VAL-CHROME-001.details: Pixel sampling at y=770-799 confirms uniform dark gradient values consistent with the background, not a chrome surface. Title bar chrome exists only at the top (y=0-34).

VAL-CHROME-002.evidence: Title bar shows: [document icon] at x≈5-25, 'Untitled' text in proportional font at x≈38-105, clock '03:27:21' at x≈1189-1265 on the right. Icon is a small multi-colored document glyph (20x24 SVG rasterized). 'Untitled' uses proportional spacing (visible character width variation). Clock uses HH:MM:SS format.
VAL-CHROME-002.details: Title bar height is 35px (y=0 to y=34, with y=35 being a bright separator line at value 75). Icon pixels show colored values (blues: 153,186,205; reds: 112,43,43; whites: 210,210,207) forming a recognizable document shape. Text and clock are legible with clear anti-aliased rendering. Serial log confirms 'parsing SVG icon' and 'SVG icon rasterized (20x24)'.

VAL-CHROME-003.evidence: Editor mode icon has document shape (continuous horizontal band at y=6-12). Image viewer icon has different shape (landscape-style overlapping rectangles at y=6-12 with gaps). 109 out of 676 pixels differ in the icon area (16% difference). Both icons render without artifacts.
VAL-CHROME-003.details: Compared pixel-by-pixel in the icon region (x=2-27, y=2-27). Editor icon shows a solid block pattern; image viewer icon shows a more complex overlapping-rectangles pattern. Serial log confirms two separate SVG icons: 'SVG icon rasterized (20x24)' and 'image icon rasterized (20x24)'.

VAL-CHROME-004.evidence: Ctrl+Tab switches from text editor (showing 'hello' text with cursor) to image viewer (showing centered PNG test image with color gradients). Ctrl+Tab again switches back to text editor (preserving 'hello' text). F1 key has no effect (image viewer remained after F1 press). Typing 'world' after switching back confirms no tab character was inserted — Ctrl+Tab does not conflict with text editing.
VAL-CHROME-004.details: Three screenshots prove the flow: (1) editor with 'hello', (2) image viewer with colorful PNG after Ctrl+Tab, (3) editor with 'hello' after second Ctrl+Tab. F1 screenshot identical to image viewer (no switch). Post-switch typing produced 'hello world' without any tab artifacts.

VAL-CHROME-005.evidence: Title bar contains only: document icon (left), 'Untitled' text (left), clock '03:27:21' (right). No 'Document OS', 'Multi-Surface Compositor', or any other branding text visible anywhere on screen. Pixel scanning of the entire title bar (y=0-34) shows only icon pixels, 'Untitled' text pixels, and clock text pixels against the chrome background.
VAL-CHROME-005.details: Full-width pixel scan of title bar at multiple y-values confirms only three content elements: icon at x≈5-25, title text at x≈38-105, and clock at x≈1189-1265. All other pixels are uniform chrome background (values 40-42).

VAL-CLK-001.evidence: Clock displayed '03:27:21' in screenshot. Host UTC time was '03:27:22' (recorded via `date -u +%H:%M:%S` immediately before screendump). Difference: 1 second, well within 60-second tolerance. Code review confirms PL031 RTC at MMIO address 0x09010000 (verified in DTB), read via `read_volatile` of the Data Register at offset 0x000 (32-bit Unix epoch seconds), converted to HH:MM:SS for display.
VAL-CLK-001.details: Code path: kernel discovers 'arm,pl031' in DTB → init forwards PA via MSG_RTC_CONFIG → compositor maps via device_map → read_rtc_seconds() reads epoch via read_volatile → converts to UTC time string. DTB confirms PL031 at PA 0x09010000. Serial log shows 'pl031 rtc mapped'.

VAL-CLK-002.evidence: Clock displays '03:27:21' in HH:MM:SS format. Hours=03 (valid 00-23), Minutes=27 (valid 00-59), Seconds=21 (valid 00-59). Multiple screenshots over ~3 minutes show clock advancing: 03:27:21 → 03:29:10 → 03:29:25 → 03:29:58 → 03:30:16 → 03:30:43 — all valid HH:MM:SS values.
VAL-CLK-002.details: Six different clock values observed across test session, all in valid HH:MM:SS format with proper ranges. Clock advances monotonically as expected from a real-time source.

VAL-CROSS-002.evidence: Radial background gradient visible through translucent title bar chrome. Title bar pixel averages by horizontal band: left edge=40.51, center=41.72, right edge=40.45. Content area gradient: left edge=19.63, center=26.52, right edge=19.63. Both follow the same center-brighter/edges-darker radial pattern, proving the gradient bleeds through the semi-transparent chrome.
VAL-CROSS-002.details: Averaged 800+ background pixels per band (excluding text/icon) across 10 horizontal bands. Title bar shows 1.2-unit center-to-edge variation matching the 7-unit variation in the content area — the ratio is consistent with alpha blending. The chrome is not fully opaque (which would show flat color); it exhibits the gradient pattern attenuated by the chrome alpha.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Chrome UI shows title bar only (no status bar), correct layout with icon/title/clock, context switching via Ctrl+Tab with icon change, no branding text. Clock displays real UTC time from PL031 RTC in valid HH:MM:SS format. Background gradient visible through translucent chrome.
